page: Amazon Web Services c9g.medium is a compute-optimized instance belonging to the c9g server family. It is powered by an AWS Graviton processor utilizing the arm64 CPU architecture. The instance features 1 dedicated physical core and 1 virtual CPU running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs. For network connectivity, the c9g.medium provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c9g.medium offers a low-resource entry point into the Graviton architecture, making it suitable for lightweight, compute-focused tasks that do not require large memory footprints or local storage. Appropriate workloads for this instance profile include small-scale web serving, microservices, lightweight containerized applications, and basic CI/CD pipeline tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c9g.medium is a compute-optimized instance powered by an arm64 AWS Graviton processor. Running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance provides 1 dedicated physical core and 1 vCPU at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Designed as a lightweight, entry-level compute node, the c9g.medium offers a resource-dense configuration for workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without massive memory requirements. It is suited for microservices, lightweight web serving, and basic containerized tasks.
